title: Adaptive proof selection shaping

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement #901 under #899 Adaptive Proof Route Selection: expose explicit proof-route decision layers while preserving current command-selection behavior.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not make learned hints host policy. Do not conflate proof intent, host policy, learned hints, target capabilities, selected commands, manual verification, and execution evidence. Keep source-repo proof commands out of host repos unless configured or discovered.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact compact/detailed projection shape and narrow tests for the explicit decision model.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The slice starts defining host quality policy, unsafe command execution, broad detector coverage, or persisted proof execution evidence beyond expectation reporting.
canonical_core.next_action: Run selected proof for #901, update closeout fields, commit, push, then stop per user instruction.
proof_expectations: uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_proof_cli.py
completion_criteria: Verbose proof output exposes proof_route_decision with distinct intent, policy, hints, live capabilities, command, fallback, and evidence-expectation layers.
Compact proof output exposes proof_next_decision-derived action, route source, command, required commands, manual fallback summary, and warnings.
Focused tests cover executable routing, stale learned hints, and manual fallback.

goal: Implement bounded committed slices for #899 Adaptive Proof Route Selection.
non_goals: Do not make learned proof route hints host policy.
Do not emit source-repo proof commands in host repos unless configured or discovered there.
Do not infer that a discovered command is sufficient proof.

intent.outcome: Implement #901 explicit proof-route decision model under #899 Adaptive Proof Route Selection.
intent.constraints: Keep decision layers separate; do not turn hints or discovered commands into host proof policy.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest additive projection that makes compact and detailed proof decisions inspectable.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the work becomes host quality policy, broad detector expansion, or proof execution persistence.

execution.next_step: Run selected proof for #901, update closeout fields, commit, push, then stop per user instruction.
execution.proof: Focused proof tests, ruff, changed-path proof, contract tooling, structured inventory, and planning summary.

invariants: Learned route hints are advisory, not host policy.
Proof selection must live-confirm route hints before using them as support for commands.

planning_mode.halt_instruction: Implementation mode active for #899 first slice; keep edits bounded to advisory proof route hints.
minimal_success_criteria: lifecycle writes advisory proof route hints
proof output live-confirms or stales learned hints
focused tests and contract checks pass
planning_mode.manual_tightening_policy: update this plan only for slice intent, proof, closeout, and continuation

required_continuation.activation trigger: After the user resumes the #899 epic after the requested stop.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Proof selection now exposes a compact next-decision projection and a detailed proof_route_decision with intent, policy, hints, live capabilities, selected commands, unavailable routes, manual fallback, and evidence expectations separated.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Host policy precedence behavior, broader detector coverage, persisted proof execution evidence, and richer manual verification templates.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Compact output must preserve existing diagnostic fields while adding the decision object so agents and tests do not lose route-adjustment evidence.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: focused proof tests and ruff passed; changed-path proof still to run before commit
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: changed-path proof selection, planning summary, contract tooling, and structured inventory before commit
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: host policy precedence or manual verification templates after the requested stop.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Stop after the current slice has been completed, committed, and pushed.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Finish #901 cleanly, push it to PR #900, and do not continue to the next #899 slice in this turn.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Current slice: explicit proof-route decision model.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Planning state, this execplan, adaptive proof decomposition, proof-selection runtime, and focused proof tests.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Keep each slice narrow enough to commit independently.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused pytest, ruff, changed-path proof selection, planning summary, contract tooling, and structured inventory.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any broad redesign that makes Agentic Workspace own host quality policy or introduces unsafe command execution.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated generated CLI work, broad detector expansion, proof execution storage, or host security/compliance policy.
execution_bounds.manual JSON validation: Do not run ad hoc JSON validation loops; use summary or package checks.

stop_conditions.stop when: The current #901 slice is completed, committed, and pushed.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The work becomes broad policy design instead of route assistance.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The slice expands beyond explicit decision projection and compact compatibility.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Focused proof, contract checks, or changed-path proof cannot validate the slice.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Implement #899 on autopilot through bounded committed slices.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Learned hints remain advisory and live-confirmed; host policy remains host-owned.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Slice boundaries, exact contract fields, tests, and proof commands.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A slice requires broad policy decisions, unsafe command execution, or host-repo quality ownership.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Run selected proof for #901, update closeout fields, commit, push, then stop per user instruction.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_proof_cli.py
completion_criteria: Verbose proof output exposes proof_route_decision with distinct intent, policy, hints, live capabilities, command, fallback, and evidence-expectation layers.
Compact proof output exposes proof_next_decision-derived action, route source, command, required commands, manual fallback summary, and warnings.
Focused tests cover executable routing, stale learned hints, and manual fallback.

execution_run.what happened: Added proof_next_decision and proof_route_decision projections, separated proof-intent/policy/hints/capability/command/fallback/evidence-expectation layers, preserved compact diagnostics, and promoted #901 in planning.
execution_run.scope touched: planning state, this execplan, adaptive proof decomposition, proof-selection runtime, and focused proof tests
execution_run.validations run: focused pytest; ruff touched proof files; planning summary; changed-path implement proof selection; agentic-planning summary --verbose; planning doctor; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; root_cli_authority defaults; tests/test_workspace_cli.py
execution_run.result for continuation: #901 implementation complete and ready to commit/push

finished_run_review.config compliance: used configured repo-local CLI invocation and selected changed-path proof
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; additive projection preserves existing behavior
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: stop after commit and push per user instruction

proof_report.validation proof: Passed: focused proof pytest, ruff touched files, planning summary, changed-path implement proof selection, agentic-planning summary --verbose, planning doctor, make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, root_cli_authority defaults, and tests/test_workspace_cli.py.
proof_report.proof achieved now: #901 explicit proof-route decision model is locally validated.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement #899 on autopilot through bounded committed slices.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #901 exposes compact and detailed proof-route decision projections with focused tests.

execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: closeout fields updated before slice commit

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The current slice implements only the explicit decision projection; host policy precedence, broader detectors, persisted execution feedback, and richer manual templates remain open in the decomposition.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: proof_next_decision, proof_route_decision, focused tests for route source, stale hint warnings, and manual fallback layers.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A host repo needs host-policy precedence, broader detectors, persisted proof execution evidence, or richer manual verification templates.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Dogfooding: stale injected thread instructions still mentioned removed --profile flags even though checked-in AGENTS.md was current.
signals fixed: Verified checked-in AGENTS.md and tests already reject --profile guidance; used the current start command without --profile.
